Course Title: Ransomware and AI in Healthcare Systems
Category: Healthcare & Life Sciences: Medical Services, Pharmacy, Biotechnology, Hospitals
Who is this for: Healthcare professionals, nurses, doctors, medical administrators, hospital IT staff, pharmacy technicians, clinical managers, healthcare support staff
Team or Dept this is for: IT, Clinical Operations, Risk Management, Information Security, Hospital Administration, Pharmacy, Medical Records
Description: Increased impact when AI-driven systems are disrupted.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with everyday healthcare technology, such as electronic patient records or hospital software systems, is all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key ways ransomware attacks target healthcare systems and explain how the disruption of AI-driven tools can amplify risks to patient safety and operational continuity. You will also be able to apply practical strategies to recognise vulnerabilities in AI-integrated healthcare environments and describe the steps organisations can take to prepare for and respond to ransomware incidents.
